body_excerpt: {"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-8428", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:H/AT:P/PR:N/UI:A/VC:H/VI:H/VA:H/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-352", "name": "Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)"}], "description": "Concrete CMS 9.5.0 and below emits a CSRF token in the local_available_update.php view ($token->output('do_update')) but the corresponding do_update() method in concrete/controllers/single_page/dashboard/system/update/update.php never calls $this->token->validate('do_update'). The form is rendered as a POST form, meaning the token reaches the browser, but because the controller discards it without verification, an attacker can craft a cross-site POST that triggers a core CMS update to an attacker-specified version string.  In order to be vulnerable, theictim must be passing canUpgrade()anda valid update version must be present under DIR_CORE_UPDATES.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00132, "percentile": 0.03055}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-prxr-vjgc-2cq9", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-24T18:39:42Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-prxr-vjgc-2cq9", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-prxr-vjgc-2cq9"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-8428"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-21T21:16:34Z", "published_at": "2026-05-21T21:30:38Z", "references": ["https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-8428", "https://documentation.concretecms.org/9-x/developers/introduction/version-history/951-release-notes", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-prxr-vjgc-2cq9"], "repository_advisory_url": null, "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/concretecms/concretecms", "summary": "Concrete CMS is Vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-24T20:57:52Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-prxr-vjgc-2cq9", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "9.5.1", "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "concrete5/concrete5"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 9.5.1"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Concrete CMS is Vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery
